title: Optic Neuritis - Multiple Sclerosis
chief_complaint: Sudden loss of vision in left eye with pain on eye movement for 3 days. Had episode of leg numbness 6 months ago that resolved spontaneously.

physical_exam.neuro: Left eye: decreased visual acuity (20/200), relative afferent pupillary defect (Marcus Gunn pupil), color desaturation. Fundoscopy: normal initially (retrobulbar neuritis)
physical_exam.motor: Mild right leg weakness (4+/5), hyperreflexia bilateral

hidden_data.mri_brain: Multiple periventricular white matter lesions (Dawson fingers), one enhancing with gadolinium, one in left optic nerve
hidden_data.lumbar_puncture: CSF: Oligoclonal bands positive (not in serum), IgG index elevated

key_learning_points: Optic neuritis = most common first presentation of MS
Oligoclonal bands in CSF but not serum

patient_responses.vision: My left eye went dark, like looking through frosted glass
patient_responses.pain: It hurts when I move my eye
patient_responses.previous: My right leg went numb 6 months ago, but it went away

feedback.correct_message: Excellent! MS diagnosed correctly - dissemination in time and space.
feedback.incorrect_message: This is MS. The optic neuritis + previous episode + MRI lesions + CSF oligoclonal bands confirm it.

mri_brain.comment: Shows Dawson fingers and enhancing lesion.

feedback.attending_note: MS acute relapse: IV methylprednisolone 1g daily for 3-5 days. Start disease-modifying therapy (ocrelizumab, natalizumab, fingolimod). Check anti-AQP4 to rule out NMO. Good job identifying the previous episode as relevant.
